summary: Joel Spolsky provides a casual trip report from his speaking tour, covering stops in Princeton, Philadelphia, and Boston. He digresses into detailed reviews of his tech setup, including his ThinkPad laptop and Samsung Blackjack phone with HSDPA tethering. He references his recent Strategy Letter and responds to readers who claimed various technologies (GWT, Flex, Silverlight, etc.) already fulfill his 'NewSDK' prediction, arguing none have achieved mainstream platform status yet. The post also mentions visiting ITA Software's Lisp-based office and closes with a humorous complaint about his hotel in Kitchener, Ontario.
key_points: The ThinkPad's trackpoint is superior to touchpads because it keeps hands on the home row and avoids accidental cursor jumps while typing
Joel uses HSDPA tethering via his Samsung Blackjack as his primary mobile internet, which he considers essential enough to delay switching to the iPhone
Readers suggested many existing technologies (GWT, Flex, Silverlight, JavaFX, etc.) as the 'NewSDK' Joel predicted, but he dismisses them all as premature contenders that lack mainstream adoption
Joel's test for a platform being truly 'here' is whether a taxi driver has heard of it, since mainstream awareness is what defines a real platform winner
ITA Software is noted as essentially the only large software shop still using Lisp in production
money_quotes: Like Windows, it's extremely frustrating and messy and disorganized. Also like Windows, if you're willing to hammer away at it, you can make it do some pretty amazing things.
These are not HERE until your TAXI DRIVER has heard of them, because I assure you he's heard of Microsoft Windows.
But GWT is no more the NewSDK than Digital Research GEM, or IBM TopView, or Quarterdeck DESQView, or Concurrent DOS, or Microsoft Windows 1.0 was the OldSDK. They're just horses at the starting gate.
That's the main reason I don't carry an iPhone — I use this HSDPA access every day and it rocks, and it's inevitable that the next gen iPhone will have it, so I'll wait, kthxbye.
If you really gave the Queen Mum these same shabby old towels as you gave me, Canada would be a republic by now.
key_insight: A technology platform isn't truly 'here' until it achieves mass mainstream awareness — being technically capable is just the starting gate, not the finish line.
